Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Xunzi?

The Xunzi (荀子) is traditionally attributed to Xunzi, also known as Xun Kuang or Xun Qing, a major Confucian philosopher of the late Warring States period in ancient China. The text is considered a compiled work, meaning it was likely gathered and edited from Xunzi's teachings and writings by his followers, similar to how other classical Chinese philosophical texts were transmitted. Xunzi is especially known for his view that human nature is originally bad and must be transformed through ritual propriety and education.
